subject: Biology
title: Stems: The Plant's Highway
description: A fast breakdown of Stems: The Plant's Highway for builders.

hook.text: Stems do more than hold leaves up. They're the transport network connecting roots to canopy.

buildup.text: Xylem carries water up. Phloem carries sugar down. Both run through the stem.

discovery.text: Woody stems add a ring of growth each year. Counting rings reveals a tree's age and climate history.

twist.text: Bamboo stems grow up to 91 centimeters in a single day—the fastest growth of any plant.

climax.text: Stems can be flexible like vines, rigid like oaks, or succulent like cacti—each adapted to its world.

punchline.text: The stem is the highway. Everything flows through it.
